description: HSL High-Frequency Positioning (HFP) â€” Helsinki Region Transport real-time vehicle telemetry. Each upstream HFP message is wrapped verbatim as a CloudEvent and re-emitted over Kafka, MQTT, and AMQP, alongside GTFS reference data (operators, routes, stops).

fi.hsl.hfp.Upstream.description: Upstream HSL HFP MQTT broker (mqtt.hsl.fi). Consumed as plain JSON on the `/hfp/v2/journey/#` topic tree; the bridge wraps each message in a CloudEvent and republishes it.

fi.hsl.hfp.Kafka.description: Downstream Kafka / Event Hubs producer for HFP telemetry on the `hsl-hfp` topic, partitioned by vehicle identity `{operator_id}/{vehicle_number}`.

fi.hsl.gtfs.operator.Kafka.description: Downstream Kafka / Event Hubs producer for the HSL operator reference catalogue on the `hsl-hfp` topic, keyed by `operator/{operator_id}`.

fi.hsl.gtfs.route.Kafka.description: Downstream Kafka / Event Hubs producer for the HSL route reference catalogue on the `hsl-hfp` topic, keyed by `route/{route_id}`.

fi.hsl.gtfs.stop.Kafka.description: Downstream Kafka / Event Hubs producer for the HSL stop reference catalogue on the `hsl-hfp` topic, keyed by `stop/{stop_id}`.

fi.hsl.Mqtt.description: Downstream MQTT 5 producer. Telemetry mirrors the upstream HFP topic tree; reference data is retained on a reference topic tree.

fi.hsl.Amqp.description: Downstream AMQP 1.0 producer (Service Bus / Event Hubs / generic brokers).

fi.hsl.hfp.description: HSL High-Frequency Positioning (HFP) vehicle telemetry. One CloudEvents type per HFP event type; all types share the vehicle identity (operator/vehicle from the topic) as subject and Kafka key.

fi.hsl.hfp.vp.name: vp
fi.hsl.hfp.vp.description: Vehicle position â€” the ~1 Hz GPS heartbeat of a vehicle on an ongoing public journey.

fi.hsl.hfp.due.name: due
fi.hsl.hfp.due.description: The vehicle will soon arrive at a stop.

fi.hsl.hfp.arr.name: arr
fi.hsl.hfp.arr.description: The vehicle has arrived inside a stop's radius.

fi.hsl.hfp.dep.name: dep
fi.hsl.hfp.dep.description: The vehicle has departed and left a stop's radius.

fi.hsl.hfp.ars.name: ars
fi.hsl.hfp.ars.description: The vehicle has arrived at a stop (stop-position event).

fi.hsl.hfp.pde.name: pde
fi.hsl.hfp.pde.description: The vehicle is ready to depart from a stop.

fi.hsl.hfp.pas.name: pas
fi.hsl.hfp.pas.description: The vehicle passes through a stop without stopping.

fi.hsl.hfp.wait.name: wait
fi.hsl.hfp.wait.description: The vehicle is waiting at a stop.

fi.hsl.hfp.doo.name: doo
fi.hsl.hfp.doo.description: The doors of the vehicle have been opened.

fi.hsl.hfp.doc.name: doc
fi.hsl.hfp.doc.description: The doors of the vehicle have been closed.

fi.hsl.hfp.vja.name: vja
fi.hsl.hfp.vja.description: The vehicle signs in to a service journey (trip).

fi.hsl.hfp.vjout.name: vjout
fi.hsl.hfp.vjout.description: The vehicle signs off from a service journey after the final stop.

fi.hsl.hfp.tlr.name: tlr
fi.hsl.hfp.tlr.description: The vehicle requests traffic-light priority at a junction.

fi.hsl.hfp.tla.name: tla
fi.hsl.hfp.tla.description: The vehicle receives a response to a traffic-light priority request.

fi.hsl.hfp.da.name: da
fi.hsl.hfp.da.description: A driver signs in to the vehicle.

fi.hsl.hfp.dout.name: dout
fi.hsl.hfp.dout.description: A driver signs out of the vehicle.

fi.hsl.hfp.ba.name: ba
fi.hsl.hfp.ba.description: A driver selects the block the vehicle will run.

fi.hsl.hfp.bout.name: bout
fi.hsl.hfp.bout.description: A driver signs out from the selected block (usually at a depot).

fi.hsl.gtfs.operator.description: HSL operator catalogue entry â€” the company that owns and operates a vehicle. Keyed by the zero-padded operator id used in the HFP topic tree. Emitted at start-up and on periodic refresh.

fi.hsl.gtfs.operator.Operator.name: Operator
fi.hsl.gtfs.operator.Operator.description: Reference record describing one HSL transit operator.

fi.hsl.gtfs.route.description: HSL GTFS route catalogue entry. Keyed by the GTFS `route_id` that appears in the HFP topic and `route` payload field. Emitted at start-up and on periodic refresh.

fi.hsl.gtfs.route.Route.name: Route
fi.hsl.gtfs.route.Route.description: Reference record describing one HSL route.

fi.hsl.gtfs.stop.description: HSL GTFS stop catalogue entry. Keyed by the GTFS `stop_id` that appears in the HFP topic `next_stop` level and `stop` payload field. Emitted at start-up and on periodic refresh.

fi.hsl.gtfs.stop.Stop.name: Stop
fi.hsl.gtfs.stop.Stop.description: Reference record describing one HSL stop or station.

fi.hsl.hfp.mqtt.description: MQTT 5 variant of the HFP telemetry events. Republished on a topic tree that mirrors the upstream HFP topic structure.

fi.hsl.hfp.amqp.description: AMQP 1.0 variant of the HFP telemetry events, with the vehicle identity as subject and routing hints in application-properties.

subject.description: AMQP message subject â€” the vehicle identity, matching the CloudEvents subject and Kafka key.

event_type.description: HFP event type carried by this message.

transport_mode.description: Vehicle transport mode from the HFP topic.

route_id.description: GTFS route id from the HFP topic; routers can filter by route without cracking the body.

operator_id.description: Owning operator id (zero-padded) from the HFP topic.

vehicle_number.description: Vehicle number (zero-padded) from the HFP topic.

fi.hsl.gtfs.operator.mqtt.description: MQTT 5 variant of the HSL operator reference events, retained so late subscribers receive the latest catalogue.

fi.hsl.gtfs.operator.amqp.description: AMQP 1.0 variant of the HSL operator reference events.

subject.description: AMQP message subject â€” prefixed reference identifier matching the CloudEvents subject and Kafka key.

fi.hsl.gtfs.route.mqtt.description: MQTT 5 variant of the HSL route reference events, retained so late subscribers receive the latest catalogue.

fi.hsl.gtfs.route.amqp.description: AMQP 1.0 variant of the HSL route reference events.

subject.description: AMQP message subject â€” prefixed reference identifier matching the CloudEvents subject and Kafka key.

fi.hsl.gtfs.stop.mqtt.description: MQTT 5 variant of the HSL stop reference events, retained so late subscribers receive the latest catalogue.

fi.hsl.gtfs.stop.amqp.description: AMQP 1.0 variant of the HSL stop reference events.

subject.description: AMQP message subject â€” prefixed reference identifier matching the CloudEvents subject and Kafka key.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.description: The upstream HSL HFP broker described as an xRegistry MQTT consumer message group â€” plain JSON payloads keyed by event type, on the verbatim HFP topic tree. Drives generation of the upstream data classes and topic helpers consumed by the bridge.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.vp.name: vp
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.vp.description: Upstream HFP `vp` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `VP` key. Vehicle position â€” the ~1 Hz GPS heartbeat of a vehicle on an ongoing public journey.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.due.name: due
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.due.description: Upstream HFP `due` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `DUE` key. The vehicle will soon arrive at a stop.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.arr.name: arr
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.arr.description: Upstream HFP `arr` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `ARR` key. The vehicle has arrived inside a stop's radius.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.dep.name: dep
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.dep.description: Upstream HFP `dep` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `DEP` key. The vehicle has departed and left a stop's radius.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.ars.name: ars
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.ars.description: Upstream HFP `ars` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `ARS` key. The vehicle has arrived at a stop (stop-position event).

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.pde.name: pde
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.pde.description: Upstream HFP `pde` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `PDE` key. The vehicle is ready to depart from a stop.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.pas.name: pas
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.pas.description: Upstream HFP `pas` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `PAS` key. The vehicle passes through a stop without stopping.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.wait.name: wait
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.wait.description: Upstream HFP `wait` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `WAIT` key. The vehicle is waiting at a stop.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.doo.name: doo
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.doo.description: Upstream HFP `doo` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `DOO` key. The doors of the vehicle have been opened.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.doc.name: doc
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.doc.description: Upstream HFP `doc` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `DOC` key. The doors of the vehicle have been closed.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.vja.name: vja
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.vja.description: Upstream HFP `vja` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `VJA` key. The vehicle signs in to a service journey (trip).

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.vjout.name: vjout
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.vjout.description: Upstream HFP `vjout` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `VJOUT` key. The vehicle signs off from a service journey after the final stop.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.tlr.name: tlr
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.tlr.description: Upstream HFP `tlr` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `TLR` key. The vehicle requests traffic-light priority at a junction.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.tla.name: tla
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.tla.description: Upstream HFP `tla` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `TLA` key. The vehicle receives a response to a traffic-light priority request.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.da.name: da
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.da.description: Upstream HFP `da` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `DA` key. A driver signs in to the vehicle.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.dout.name: dout
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.dout.description: Upstream HFP `dout` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `DOUT` key. A driver signs out of the vehicle.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.ba.name: ba
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.ba.description: Upstream HFP `ba` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `BA` key. A driver selects the block the vehicle will run.

fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.bout.name: bout
fi.hsl.hfp.upstream.bout.description: Upstream HFP `bout` message as published by HSL on `mqtt.hsl.fi` â€” plain JSON (no CloudEvents envelope), with the event object under the single `BOUT` key. A driver signs out from the selected block (usually at a depot).

VehicleEvent.name: VehicleEvent
VehicleEvent.description: Telemetry payload shared by the HFP vehicle-position event (`vp`), the stop progress events (`due`,`arr`,`dep`,`ars`,`pde`,`pas`,`wait`), the door events (`doo`,`doc`) and the service-journey sign events (`vja`,`vjout`). Mirrors the single HFP v2 payload field table verbatim (the upstream defines one payload object whose populated fields depend on the event type, identified by the CloudEvents `type`). All fields except the operator/vehicle/timestamp identity are optional because their presence depends on the event type and on data availability.

oper.description: Unique numeric ID of the operator *running* the trip. This MAY differ from the `operator_id` MQTT-topic level (the *owning* operator) when a service is subcontracted to another operator. Carries no leading zeroes. Sourced from the HFP payload `oper` field. Resolve against the operator catalogue (`fi.hsl.gtfs.Operator`).

veh.description: Vehicle number painted on the side of the vehicle (often next to the front door). Unique only in combination with the operator; different operators may reuse vehicle numbers. Matches the `vehicle_number` topic level without its leading zeroes. Sourced from the HFP payload `veh` field.

tst.description: UTC timestamp with millisecond precision generated by the vehicle, in ISO 8601 `yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'`. Sourced from the HFP payload `tst` field. Also surfaced as the CloudEvents `time` attribute.

tsi.description: POSIX/Unix time in whole seconds from the vehicle, equal to `tst` truncated to seconds. Sourced from the HFP payload `tsi` field. Modeled as a 32-bit integer so it round-trips as a JSON number (verbatim with the upstream) rather than the stringified form avrotize emits for 64-bit integers; the value stays within int32 range until 2038-01-19.

operator_id.description: Zero-padded 4-digit ID of the *owning* operator, taken from the `operator_id` MQTT-topic level (e.g. `0055`, `0012`). The stable vehicle-owner identifier (unlike the mutable payload `oper`, which is the *running* operator under subcontracting). Forms the first segment of the CloudEvents subject and Kafka key; resolve against `fi.hsl.gtfs.Operator`.

vehicle_number.description: Zero-padded vehicle number (4-5 digits) taken from the `vehicle_number` MQTT-topic level (e.g. `01216`). Unique only together with `operator_id`; matches the payload `veh` field without its leading zeroes. Forms the second segment of the CloudEvents subject and Kafka key.

temporal_type.description: Journey temporal type from the MQTT-topic level: `ongoing` (journey in progress) or `upcoming` (journey about to start). The bridge subscribes to both; the `deadrun`/`signoff` trees are not consumed.

transport_mode.description: Vehicle transport mode from the MQTT-topic level (`bus`, `tram`, `train`, `ferry`, `metro`, `ubus`, `robot`).

route_id.description: GTFS route ID from the `route_id` MQTT-topic level (without the `HSL:` prefix), e.g. `2550`. The topic twin of the payload `route` field; `null` when the topic level is empty. Resolve against `fi.hsl.gtfs.Route`.

direction_id.description: Route direction from the `direction_id` MQTT-topic level, `1` or `2` â€” the topic twin of the payload `dir` field.

headsign.description: Vehicle headsign from the MQTT-topic level â€” the destination shown on the vehicle, e.g. `Eira`. May be empty on the topic. Free-form text.

start_time.description: Scheduled start time of the trip from the `start_time` MQTT-topic level, in `HH:mm` 24-hour local time â€” the topic twin of the payload `start` field.

next_stop.description: Next stop ID from the `next_stop` MQTT-topic level, e.g. `1130106`. The topic carries the literal `EOL` at the end of the line and may be empty, so this is a free-form string (not a numeric stop ID). Resolve numeric values against `fi.hsl.gtfs.Stop`.

geohash_level.description: Geohash precision level (0-5) from the MQTT-topic level, indicating how many leading digits of the vehicle position have changed since the previous message. Carried as a string to mirror the topic verbatim.

geohash.description: Slash-delimited geohash of the vehicle position assembled from the trailing MQTT-topic levels (e.g. `60;24/18/71/93`), used by HSL for geographic topic filtering. Carried as a string to mirror the topic verbatim.

desi.description: Route number visible to passengers â€” the public-facing line label shown on the head sign, e.g. `551`, `H72`. Sourced from the HFP payload `desi` field. Not populated on driver/block sign events (`da`,`dout`,`ba`,`bout`).

dir.description: Route direction of the trip as a string, either `"1"` or `"2"`. After type conversion this matches the `direction_id` topic level; relative to GTFS it is offset by one (HFP `1` = GTFS `0`, HFP `2` = GTFS `1`). Sourced from the HFP payload `dir` field. Not populated on `da`,`dout`,`ba`,`bout`.

dl.description: Offset from the scheduled timetable in seconds (s). Negative values indicate the vehicle is lagging behind schedule, positive values indicate running ahead. Sourced from the HFP payload `dl` field. Not populated on `da`,`dout`,`ba`,`bout`.

oday.description: Operating day of the trip in `YYYY-MM-DD`. The operating day typically ends around 04:30 local time on the following calendar day, so the final moments of day `2018-04-05` fall at 2018-04-06T04:30 local. Sourced from the HFP payload `oday` field. Not populated on `da`,`dout`.

jrn.description: Internal journey descriptor used by HSL. Not meant to be useful for external consumers. Sourced from the HFP payload `jrn` field.

line.description: Internal line descriptor used by HSL. Not meant to be useful for external consumers. Sourced from the HFP payload `line` field.

start.description: Scheduled start time of the trip â€” the scheduled departure from the first stop â€” in `HH:mm` 24-hour local time (not the 30-hour GTFS operating-day clock). Matches the `start_time` topic level. Sourced from the HFP payload `start` field.

stop.description: Numeric GTFS stop ID of the stop related to this event (for example the stop the vehicle departed from for a `dep` event, or the stop it currently sits at for a `vp` event). `null` when the event is not related to any stop. Equals `stop_id` in GTFS without the `HSL:` prefix; resolve against `fi.hsl.gtfs.Stop`. Sourced from the HFP payload `stop` field, which is a JSON number on the wire (the upstream field table mislabels it as a String; live traffic confirms an integer or null).

route.description: GTFS route ID the vehicle is currently running on. Matches the `route_id` topic level and `route_id` in GTFS without the `HSL:` prefix; resolve against `fi.hsl.gtfs.Route`. Sourced from the HFP payload `route` field. Not populated on `da`,`dout`,`ba`,`bout`.

occu.description: Passenger occupancy level on the interval [0, 100]. Currently only Suomenlinna ferries report a measured value; other vehicles emit `0` (space available, accepting passengers) or `100` (full, may not accept passengers). Sourced from the HFP payload `occu` field.

seq.description: Sequence number of the unit within a multi-unit consist (e.g. coupled metro or train units), starting at 1. Currently only populated for metros. Sourced from the HFP payload `seq` field.

label.description: Human-visible label that helps identify the vehicle. Currently only Suomenlinna ferries populate this, with the vessel name. Sourced from the HFP payload `label` field.

spd.description: Instantaneous ground speed of the vehicle in metres per second (m/s). Sourced from the HFP payload `spd` field.

hdg.description: Heading of the vehicle in degrees (Â°) clockwise from geographic north, on the closed interval [0, 360]. Sourced from the HFP payload `hdg` field.

lat.description: WGS84 latitude of the vehicle in decimal degrees (Â°). `null` when the vehicle location is unavailable. Sourced from the HFP payload `lat` field.

long.description: WGS84 longitude of the vehicle in decimal degrees (Â°). `null` when the vehicle location is unavailable. Sourced from the HFP payload `long` field (the upstream key is literally `long`).

acc.description: Acceleration in metres per second squared (m/s^2), derived from the speed delta between this and the previous message. Negative values indicate the vehicle is decelerating. Sourced from the HFP payload `acc` field.

odo.description: Odometer reading in metres (m) since the start of the trip. The upstream notes the value is currently not very reliable. Sourced from the HFP payload `odo` field.

drst.description: Door status. `0` = all doors closed, `1` = at least one door open. `null` when unknown. Sourced from the HFP payload `drst` field.

loc.description: Source of the reported location. One of `GPS` (satellite fix), `ODO` (computed from the odometer), `MAN` (manually set), `DR` (dead reckoning, used in tunnels and other GPS-denied locations) or `N/A` (location unavailable). Sourced from the HFP payload `loc` field.

ttarr.description: UTC timestamp of the scheduled arrival time to the stop related to a stop event, ISO 8601. Populated on the stop and door events (`due`,`arr`,`dep`,`ars`,`pde`,`pas`,`wait`,`doo`,`doc`); absent on `vp` and on the service-journey sign events. Sourced from the HFP payload `ttarr` field.

ttdep.description: UTC timestamp of the scheduled departure time from the stop related to a stop event, ISO 8601. Populated on the stop and door events; absent on `vp` and on the service-journey sign events. Sourced from the HFP payload `ttdep` field.

dr_type.description: Type of the driver. `0` = service technician, `1` = normal driver. Populated on the service-journey sign events (`vja`,`vjout`). Sourced from the HFP payload `dr-type` field.

TemporalTypeEnum.name: TemporalTypeEnum
TemporalTypeEnum.description: Journey temporal type: `ongoing` (journey currently in progress) or `upcoming` (journey about to start, within ~30 minutes). From the MQTT-topic `temporal_type` level.

TransportModeEnum.name: TransportModeEnum
TransportModeEnum.description: Transport mode of the vehicle: `bus`, `tram`, `train`, `ferry`, `metro`, `ubus` (U-line bus) or `robot` (robot bus). From the MQTT-topic `transport_mode` level.

TrafficLightEvent.name: TrafficLightEvent
TrafficLightEvent.description: Telemetry payload for the traffic-light priority events: the request (`tlr`) and the response (`tla`). Carries the vehicle identity, time and position context plus the `tlp-*`, `sid` and `signal-groupid` priority fields. Mirrors the HFP v2 payload field table for these event types.

oper.description: Unique numeric ID of the operator *running* the trip. This MAY differ from the `operator_id` MQTT-topic level (the *owning* operator) when a service is subcontracted to another operator. Carries no leading zeroes. Sourced from the HFP payload `oper` field. Resolve against the operator catalogue (`fi.hsl.gtfs.Operator`).

veh.description: Vehicle number painted on the side of the vehicle (often next to the front door). Unique only in combination with the operator; different operators may reuse vehicle numbers. Matches the `vehicle_number` topic level without its leading zeroes. Sourced from the HFP payload `veh` field.

tst.description: UTC timestamp with millisecond precision generated by the vehicle, in ISO 8601 `yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'`. Sourced from the HFP payload `tst` field. Also surfaced as the CloudEvents `time` attribute.

tsi.description: POSIX/Unix time in whole seconds from the vehicle, equal to `tst` truncated to seconds. Sourced from the HFP payload `tsi` field. Modeled as a 32-bit integer so it round-trips as a JSON number (verbatim with the upstream) rather than the stringified form avrotize emits for 64-bit integers; the value stays within int32 range until 2038-01-19.

operator_id.description: Zero-padded 4-digit ID of the *owning* operator, taken from the `operator_id` MQTT-topic level (e.g. `0055`, `0012`). The stable vehicle-owner identifier (unlike the mutable payload `oper`, which is the *running* operator under subcontracting). Forms the first segment of the CloudEvents subject and Kafka key; resolve against `fi.hsl.gtfs.Operator`.

vehicle_number.description: Zero-padded vehicle number (4-5 digits) taken from the `vehicle_number` MQTT-topic level (e.g. `01216`). Unique only together with `operator_id`; matches the payload `veh` field without its leading zeroes. Forms the second segment of the CloudEvents subject and Kafka key.

temporal_type.description: Journey temporal type from the MQTT-topic level: `ongoing` (journey in progress) or `upcoming` (journey about to start). The bridge subscribes to both; the `deadrun`/`signoff` trees are not consumed.

transport_mode.description: Vehicle transport mode from the MQTT-topic level (`bus`, `tram`, `train`, `ferry`, `metro`, `ubus`, `robot`).

route_id.description: GTFS route ID from the `route_id` MQTT-topic level (without the `HSL:` prefix), e.g. `2550`. The topic twin of the payload `route` field; `null` when the topic level is empty. Resolve against `fi.hsl.gtfs.Route`.

direction_id.description: Route direction from the `direction_id` MQTT-topic level, `1` or `2` â€” the topic twin of the payload `dir` field.

headsign.description: Vehicle headsign from the MQTT-topic level â€” the destination shown on the vehicle, e.g. `Eira`. May be empty on the topic. Free-form text.

start_time.description: Scheduled start time of the trip from the `start_time` MQTT-topic level, in `HH:mm` 24-hour local time â€” the topic twin of the payload `start` field.

next_stop.description: Next stop ID from the `next_stop` MQTT-topic level, e.g. `1130106`. The topic carries the literal `EOL` at the end of the line and may be empty, so this is a free-form string (not a numeric stop ID). Resolve numeric values against `fi.hsl.gtfs.Stop`.

geohash_level.description: Geohash precision level (0-5) from the MQTT-topic level, indicating how many leading digits of the vehicle position have changed since the previous message. Carried as a string to mirror the topic verbatim.

geohash.description: Slash-delimited geohash of the vehicle position assembled from the trailing MQTT-topic levels (e.g. `60;24/18/71/93`), used by HSL for geographic topic filtering. Carried as a string to mirror the topic verbatim.

desi.description: Route number visible to passengers â€” the public-facing line label shown on the head sign, e.g. `551`, `H72`. Sourced from the HFP payload `desi` field. Not populated on driver/block sign events (`da`,`dout`,`ba`,`bout`).

dir.description: Route direction of the trip as a string, either `"1"` or `"2"`. After type conversion this matches the `direction_id` topic level; relative to GTFS it is offset by one (HFP `1` = GTFS `0`, HFP `2` = GTFS `1`). Sourced from the HFP payload `dir` field. Not populated on `da`,`dout`,`ba`,`bout`.

dl.description: Offset from the scheduled timetable in seconds (s). Negative values indicate the vehicle is lagging behind schedule, positive values indicate running ahead. Sourced from the HFP payload `dl` field. Not populated on `da`,`dout`,`ba`,`bout`.

oday.description: Operating day of the trip in `YYYY-MM-DD`. The operating day typically ends around 04:30 local time on the following calendar day, so the final moments of day `2018-04-05` fall at 2018-04-06T04:30 local. Sourced from the HFP payload `oday` field. Not populated on `da`,`dout`.

jrn.description: Internal journey descriptor used by HSL. Not meant to be useful for external consumers. Sourced from the HFP payload `jrn` field.

line.description: Internal line descriptor used by HSL. Not meant to be useful for external consumers. Sourced from the HFP payload `line` field.

start.description: Scheduled start time of the trip â€” the scheduled departure from the first stop â€” in `HH:mm` 24-hour local time (not the 30-hour GTFS operating-day clock). Matches the `start_time` topic level. Sourced from the HFP payload `start` field.

stop.description: Numeric GTFS stop ID of the stop related to this event (for example the stop the vehicle departed from for a `dep` event, or the stop it currently sits at for a `vp` event). `null` when the event is not related to any stop. Equals `stop_id` in GTFS without the `HSL:` prefix; resolve against `fi.hsl.gtfs.Stop`. Sourced from the HFP payload `stop` field, which is a JSON number on the wire (the upstream field table mislabels it as a String; live traffic confirms an integer or null).

route.description: GTFS route ID the vehicle is currently running on. Matches the `route_id` topic level and `route_id` in GTFS without the `HSL:` prefix; resolve against `fi.hsl.gtfs.Route`. Sourced from the HFP payload `route` field. Not populated on `da`,`dout`,`ba`,`bout`.

occu.description: Passenger occupancy level on the interval [0, 100]. Currently only Suomenlinna ferries report a measured value; other vehicles emit `0` (space available, accepting passengers) or `100` (full, may not accept passengers). Sourced from the HFP payload `occu` field.

spd.description: Instantaneous ground speed of the vehicle in metres per second (m/s). Sourced from the HFP payload `spd` field.

hdg.description: Heading of the vehicle in degrees (Â°) clockwise from geographic north, on the closed interval [0, 360]. Sourced from the HFP payload `hdg` field.

lat.description: WGS84 latitude of the vehicle in decimal degrees (Â°). `null` when the vehicle location is unavailable. Sourced from the HFP payload `lat` field.

long.description: WGS84 longitude of the vehicle in decimal degrees (Â°). `null` when the vehicle location is unavailable. Sourced from the HFP payload `long` field (the upstream key is literally `long`).

acc.description: Acceleration in metres per second squared (m/s^2), derived from the speed delta between this and the previous message. Negative values indicate the vehicle is decelerating. Sourced from the HFP payload `acc` field.

odo.description: Odometer reading in metres (m) since the start of the trip. The upstream notes the value is currently not very reliable. Sourced from the HFP payload `odo` field.

drst.description: Door status. `0` = all doors closed, `1` = at least one door open. `null` when unknown. Sourced from the HFP payload `drst` field.

loc.description: Source of the reported location. One of `GPS` (satellite fix), `ODO` (computed from the odometer), `MAN` (manually set), `DR` (dead reckoning, used in tunnels and other GPS-denied locations) or `N/A` (location unavailable). Sourced from the HFP payload `loc` field.

tlp_requestid.description: Traffic-light priority request ID on the interval [0, 255]. Populated on `tlr` (the request) and echoed on `tla` (the response). Sourced from the HFP payload `tlp-requestid` field.

tlp_requesttype.description: Type of the traffic-light priority request. One of `NORMAL`, `DOOR_CLOSE`, `DOOR_OPEN` or `ADVANCE`. Populated on `tlr`. Sourced from the HFP payload `tlp-requesttype` field.

tlp_prioritylevel.description: Priority level of the traffic-light priority request. One of `normal`, `high` or `norequest`. Populated on `tlr`. Sourced from the HFP payload `tlp-prioritylevel` field.

tlp_reason.description: Reason for *not* sending a traffic-light priority request. One of `GLOBAL`, `AHEAD`, `LINE` or `PRIOEXEP`. Populated on `tlr`. Sourced from the HFP payload `tlp-reason` field.

tlp_att_seq.description: Traffic-light priority request attempt sequence number. Populated on `tlr`. Sourced from the HFP payload `tlp-att-seq` field.

tlp_decision.description: Response decision for the traffic-light priority request. One of `ACK` or `NAK`. Populated on `tla` (the response). Sourced from the HFP payload `tlp-decision` field.

sid.description: Junction ID where priority is requested, matching the `sid` topic level. Populated on `tlr` and `tla`. Sourced from the HFP payload `sid` field.

signal_groupid.description: Signal-group ID (a group of traffic lights at a junction). Populated on `tlr`. Sourced from the HFP payload `signal-groupid` field.

tlp_signalgroupnbr.description: ID of the specific traffic light within the signal group; may be negative. Populated on `tlr`. Sourced from the HFP payload `tlp-signalgroupnbr` field.

tlp_line_configid.description: ID of the line configuration in DOI. Populated on `tlr`. Sourced from the HFP payload `tlp-line-configid` field.

tlp_point_configid.description: Point configuration ID. Populated on `tlr`. Sourced from the HFP payload `tlp-point-configid` field.

tlp_frequency.description: Frequency used for the traffic-light priority request. Populated on `tlr`. Sourced from the HFP payload `tlp-frequency` field.

tlp_protocol.description: Protocol used for the traffic-light priority request. One of `MQTT` or `KAR-MQTT`. Populated on `tlr`. Sourced from the HFP payload `tlp-protocol` field.

TemporalTypeEnum.name: TemporalTypeEnum
TemporalTypeEnum.description: Journey temporal type: `ongoing` (journey currently in progress) or `upcoming` (journey about to start, within ~30 minutes). From the MQTT-topic `temporal_type` level.

TlpDecisionEnum.name: TlpDecisionEnum
TlpDecisionEnum.description: Response decision for the traffic-light priority request: `ACK` (granted) or `NAK` (denied). From the HFP payload `tlp-decision` field.

TlpPriorityLevelEnum.name: TlpPriorityLevelEnum
TlpPriorityLevelEnum.description: Priority level of the traffic-light priority request: `normal`, `high` or `norequest` (no request sent). From the HFP payload `tlp-prioritylevel` field.

TlpReasonEnum.name: TlpReasonEnum
TlpReasonEnum.description: Reason for *not* sending a traffic-light priority request: `GLOBAL`, `AHEAD`, `LINE` or `PRIOEXEP`. From the HFP payload `tlp-reason` field.

TlpRequestTypeEnum.name: TlpRequestTypeEnum
TlpRequestTypeEnum.description: Type of the traffic-light priority request: `NORMAL`, `DOOR_CLOSE`, `DOOR_OPEN` or `ADVANCE`. From the HFP payload `tlp-requesttype` field.

TransportModeEnum.name: TransportModeEnum
TransportModeEnum.description: Transport mode of the vehicle: `bus`, `tram`, `train`, `ferry`, `metro`, `ubus` (U-line bus) or `robot` (robot bus). From the MQTT-topic `transport_mode` level.

DriverBlockEvent.name: DriverBlockEvent
DriverBlockEvent.description: Telemetry payload for the low-frequency driver and block sign events: driver sign-in (`da`), driver sign-out (`dout`), block selection (`ba`) and block sign-out (`bout`). These carry a reduced field set â€” vehicle identity, time, position and driver type â€” because route, schedule and stop context are not available when the vehicle is not signed on to a public journey.

oper.description: Unique numeric ID of the operator *running* the trip. This MAY differ from the `operator_id` MQTT-topic level (the *owning* operator) when a service is subcontracted to another operator. Carries no leading zeroes. Sourced from the HFP payload `oper` field. Resolve against the operator catalogue (`fi.hsl.gtfs.Operator`).

veh.description: Vehicle number painted on the side of the vehicle (often next to the front door). Unique only in combination with the operator; different operators may reuse vehicle numbers. Matches the `vehicle_number` topic level without its leading zeroes. Sourced from the HFP payload `veh` field.

tst.description: UTC timestamp with millisecond precision generated by the vehicle, in ISO 8601 `yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSS'Z'`. Sourced from the HFP payload `tst` field. Also surfaced as the CloudEvents `time` attribute.

tsi.description: POSIX/Unix time in whole seconds from the vehicle, equal to `tst` truncated to seconds. Sourced from the HFP payload `tsi` field. Modeled as a 32-bit integer so it round-trips as a JSON number (verbatim with the upstream) rather than the stringified form avrotize emits for 64-bit integers; the value stays within int32 range until 2038-01-19.

operator_id.description: Zero-padded 4-digit ID of the *owning* operator, taken from the `operator_id` MQTT-topic level (e.g. `0055`, `0012`). The stable vehicle-owner identifier (unlike the mutable payload `oper`, which is the *running* operator under subcontracting). Forms the first segment of the CloudEvents subject and Kafka key; resolve against `fi.hsl.gtfs.Operator`.

vehicle_number.description: Zero-padded vehicle number (4-5 digits) taken from the `vehicle_number` MQTT-topic level (e.g. `01216`). Unique only together with `operator_id`; matches the payload `veh` field without its leading zeroes. Forms the second segment of the CloudEvents subject and Kafka key.

temporal_type.description: Journey temporal type from the MQTT-topic level: `ongoing` (journey in progress) or `upcoming` (journey about to start). The bridge subscribes to both; the `deadrun`/`signoff` trees are not consumed.

transport_mode.description: Vehicle transport mode from the MQTT-topic level (`bus`, `tram`, `train`, `ferry`, `metro`, `ubus`, `robot`).

route_id.description: GTFS route ID from the `route_id` MQTT-topic level (without the `HSL:` prefix), e.g. `2550`. The topic twin of the payload `route` field; `null` when the topic level is empty. Resolve against `fi.hsl.gtfs.Route`.

direction_id.description: Route direction from the `direction_id` MQTT-topic level, `1` or `2` â€” the topic twin of the payload `dir` field.

headsign.description: Vehicle headsign from the MQTT-topic level â€” the destination shown on the vehicle, e.g. `Eira`. May be empty on the topic. Free-form text.

start_time.description: Scheduled start time of the trip from the `start_time` MQTT-topic level, in `HH:mm` 24-hour local time â€” the topic twin of the payload `start` field.

next_stop.description: Next stop ID from the `next_stop` MQTT-topic level, e.g. `1130106`. The topic carries the literal `EOL` at the end of the line and may be empty, so this is a free-form string (not a numeric stop ID). Resolve numeric values against `fi.hsl.gtfs.Stop`.

geohash_level.description: Geohash precision level (0-5) from the MQTT-topic level, indicating how many leading digits of the vehicle position have changed since the previous message. Carried as a string to mirror the topic verbatim.

geohash.description: Slash-delimited geohash of the vehicle position assembled from the trailing MQTT-topic levels (e.g. `60;24/18/71/93`), used by HSL for geographic topic filtering. Carried as a string to mirror the topic verbatim.

spd.description: Instantaneous ground speed of the vehicle in metres per second (m/s). Sourced from the HFP payload `spd` field.

hdg.description: Heading of the vehicle in degrees (Â°) clockwise from geographic north, on the closed interval [0, 360]. Sourced from the HFP payload `hdg` field.

lat.description: WGS84 latitude of the vehicle in decimal degrees (Â°). `null` when the vehicle location is unavailable. Sourced from the HFP payload `lat` field.

long.description: WGS84 longitude of the vehicle in decimal degrees (Â°). `null` when the vehicle location is unavailable. Sourced from the HFP payload `long` field (the upstream key is literally `long`).

acc.description: Acceleration in metres per second squared (m/s^2), derived from the speed delta between this and the previous message. Negative values indicate the vehicle is decelerating. Sourced from the HFP payload `acc` field.

odo.description: Odometer reading in metres (m) since the start of the trip. The upstream notes the value is currently not very reliable. Sourced from the HFP payload `odo` field.

drst.description: Door status. `0` = all doors closed, `1` = at least one door open. `null` when unknown. Sourced from the HFP payload `drst` field.

loc.description: Source of the reported location. One of `GPS` (satellite fix), `ODO` (computed from the odometer), `MAN` (manually set), `DR` (dead reckoning, used in tunnels and other GPS-denied locations) or `N/A` (location unavailable). Sourced from the HFP payload `loc` field.

oday.description: Operating day of the trip in `YYYY-MM-DD`. Populated on the block events `ba`/`bout`; not populated on the driver sign events `da`/`dout`. Sourced from the HFP payload `oday` field.

dr_type.description: Type of the driver. `0` = service technician, `1` = normal driver. Sourced from the HFP payload `dr-type` field.

TemporalTypeEnum.name: TemporalTypeEnum
TemporalTypeEnum.description: Journey temporal type: `ongoing` (journey currently in progress) or `upcoming` (journey about to start, within ~30 minutes). From the MQTT-topic `temporal_type` level.

TransportModeEnum.name: TransportModeEnum
TransportModeEnum.description: Transport mode of the vehicle: `bus`, `tram`, `train`, `ferry`, `metro`, `ubus` (U-line bus) or `robot` (robot bus). From the MQTT-topic `transport_mode` level.

Operator.name: Operator
Operator.description: Reference record for one HSL transit operator, derived from the HFP operator catalogue. Emitted at bridge start-up and refreshed periodically so consumers can resolve the `operator_id` topic level and the payload `oper` field to a named operator without an out-of-band lookup.

operator_id.description: Zero-padded 4-digit ID of the *owning* operator, matching the `operator_id` MQTT-topic level (e.g. `0012`, `0060`). Stable identifier; used as the CloudEvents subject and Kafka key (prefixed `operator/`) for this reference record. Derived from the HSL HFP operator catalogue.

operator_number.description: Numeric operator ID without leading zeroes, matching the HFP payload `oper` field form (e.g. `12`, `60`). Note operator `6` appears only in the payload `oper` field and denotes the same owner as `18`. Derived from the HSL HFP operator catalogue.

name.description: Operator (transit company) name as published in the HSL HFP operator catalogue, e.g. `Koiviston Auto Oy`, `Suomenlinnan Liikenne Oy`.

note.description: Free-form note from the operator catalogue, e.g. that multiple smaller operators run under a shared operator ID. `null` when there is no note.

Route.name: Route
Route.description: Reference record for one HSL route, sourced from the HSL GTFS static `routes.txt`. Emitted at bridge start-up and refreshed periodically so consumers can resolve the HFP `route` field / `route_id` topic level to a named route and transit mode. Shares the vehicle-telemetry Kafka topic so a consumer can build a temporally consistent view of telemetry and the routes it references.

route_id.description: GTFS route identifier without the `HSL:` prefix, matching the HFP payload `route` field and the `route_id` topic level (e.g. `2550`). Stable identifier; used as the CloudEvents subject and Kafka key (prefixed `route/`). Sourced from `route_id` in the HSL GTFS `routes.txt`.

agency_id.description: Identifier of the agency that operates the route, a foreign key into the GTFS `agency.txt`. Sourced from the `agency_id` field of `routes.txt`.

route_short_name.description: Short, public-facing route name shown to riders (e.g. `550`, `H72`); frequently equals the HFP `desi` field. Sourced from the `route_short_name` field of `routes.txt`.

route_long_name.description: Full, descriptive route name, e.g. `ItĂ¤keskus(M) - Westendinasema`. Sourced from the `route_long_name` field of `routes.txt`.

route_desc.description: Optional human-readable description providing useful information about the route. Sourced from the `route_desc` field of `routes.txt`.

route_type.description: GTFS route type / extended Hierarchical Vehicle Type (HVT) describing the transit mode used on the route. HSL emits both the basic GTFS values â€” `0` = tram / light rail, `1` = subway / metro, `4` = ferry â€” and the extended HVT codes â€” `109` = suburban railway, `700`/`701` = bus, `702` = express bus, `704` = local bus, `900` = funicular. Modeled as an open integer (not a closed enum) because HSL may introduce further HVT codes. Sourced from the `route_type` field of `routes.txt`.

route_url.description: URL of a web page about the route. Sourced from the `route_url` field of `routes.txt`.

Stop.name: Stop
Stop.description: Reference record for one HSL stop, station or station entrance, sourced from the HSL GTFS static `stops.txt`. Emitted at bridge start-up and refreshed periodically so consumers can resolve the HFP `stop` field / `next_stop` topic level to a named, geolocated stop. Shares the vehicle-telemetry Kafka topic for temporal consistency with the telemetry it contextualises.

stop_id.description: GTFS stop identifier without the `HSL:` prefix, matching the HFP payload `stop` field and the `next_stop` topic level (e.g. `1130106`). Stable identifier; used as the CloudEvents subject and Kafka key (prefixed `stop/`). Sourced from `stop_id` in the HSL GTFS `stops.txt`.

stop_code.description: Short text or number that uniquely identifies the stop for riders (shown on signage and in journey planners), e.g. `H1234`. Sourced from the `stop_code` field of `stops.txt`.

stop_name.description: Name of the stop, station, or station entrance as shown to riders, e.g. `Rautatientori`. Sourced from the `stop_name` field of `stops.txt`.

stop_desc.description: Description providing useful, quality information about the stop. Sourced from the `stop_desc` field of `stops.txt`.

stop_lat.description: WGS84 latitude of the stop in decimal degrees (Â°), on [-90, 90]. Sourced from the `stop_lat` field of `stops.txt`.

stop_lon.description: WGS84 longitude of the stop in decimal degrees (Â°), on [-180, 180]. Sourced from the `stop_lon` field of `stops.txt`.

zone_id.description: HSL fare zone the stop belongs to, e.g. `A`, `B`, `C`, `D`. Sourced from the `zone_id` field of `stops.txt`.

stop_url.description: URL of a web page about the stop. Sourced from the `stop_url` field of `stops.txt`.

location_type.description: Type of the location. `0` (or empty) = stop / platform, `1` = station, `2` = station entrance / exit, `3` = generic node, `4` = boarding area. Sourced from the `location_type` field of `stops.txt`.

parent_station.description: `stop_id` of the parent station for a platform, entrance or boarding area. Sourced from the `parent_station` field of `stops.txt`.

platform_code.description: Platform identifier for a platform stop (the part that differs between platforms of a station), e.g. `1`, `B`. Sourced from the `platform_code` field of `stops.txt`.

wheelchair_boarding.description: Wheelchair accessibility of boarding at the stop. `0` (or empty) = no information, `1` = some accessible boarding, `2` = no accessible boarding. Sourced from the `wheelchair_boarding` field of `stops.txt`.

vehicle_type.description: HSL extension: the Hierarchical Vehicle Type (extended GTFS route type) of the vehicles that serve this stop, e.g. `0` = tram, `1` = subway, `109` = suburban rail, `700`/`704` = bus, `4` = ferry. Empty when not assigned. Sourced from the non-standard `vehicle_type` field of the HSL `stops.txt`.

digistop_id.description: HSL extension: internal DigiStop identifier for the stop. Sourced from the non-standard `digistop_id` field of the HSL `stops.txt`. `null` when not present.
